selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As an 18-year-old non-binary hairdresser from Kansas City, I've had my share of chats about healthcare and COVID vaccines while styling hair or just hanging out. Being a widow at a young age has made me think a lot about health and safety, and my Christian beliefs guide me to care for others, even when I'm feeling depressed. I don't use social media, so I rely on real conversations and what I learn from clients and friends. In my job, I hear all sorts of opinions, and I've found that listening first is key. With my high extraversion, I enjoy talking to people, but my moderate agreeableness means I don't always back down if I disagree. For managing these discussions, I focus on being respectful and sharing my own experiences. For example, I might talk about how getting vaccinated helped me feel safer at work, where I'm around people all day. I also draw on my hobbies like running and birdwatching to connect health topics to everyday life, like how staying active boosts my mood. Since I have no formal education, I keep things simple and avoid jargon, sticking to what I know from personal stories and trusted sources like my doctor or community leaders. I believe in balancing facts with empathy, especially since I understand depression and how stressful health talks can be. My approach is to stay calm, ask questions to understand others' views, and gently share why vaccines matter to me, without pushing too hard. It's about building trust and keeping the conversation open, so everyone feels heard.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 18 and occupation as hairdresser suggest frequent social interactions, influencing discussion management. Gender non-binary and Christian beliefs add perspective on inclusivity and care. Widowed status and depression relate to personal health concerns, emphasizing empathy. No social media use implies reliance on direct conversations. High extraversion supports engagement in talks, moderate agreeableness allows for respectful disagreement. Hobbies like running tie to health advocacy. No formal education leads to simple, experience-based communication. Overall, focused on listening, sharing personal stories, and balancing facts with emotional sensitivity.",
  "tone": "Conversational, empathetic, and straightforward, reflecting a young adult's perspective with personal anecdotes and a focus on real-world interactions.",
  "citations": ["none"],
  "safety_notes": "none",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "occupation", "gender", "religious_beliefs", "marital_status", "psychological_feature", "time_onsocialmedia", "extraversion", "agreeableness", "hobbies", "education_level"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As an 18-year-old non-binary hairdresser from Kansas City, I've had my share of chats about healthcare and COVID vaccines while styling hair or just hanging out. Being a widow at a young age has made me think a lot about health and safety, and my Christian beliefs guide me to care for others, even when I'm feeling depressed. I don't use social media, so I rely on real conversations and what I learn from clients and friends. In my job, I hear all sorts of opinions, and I've found that listening first is key. With my high extraversion, I enjoy talking to people, but my moderate agreeableness means I don't always back down if I disagree. For managing these discussions, I focus on being respectful and sharing my own experiences. For example, I might talk about how getting vaccinated helped me feel safer at work, where I'm around people all day. I also draw on my hobbies like running and birdwatching to connect health topics to everyday life, like how staying active boosts my mood. Since I have no formal education, I keep things simple and avoid jargon, sticking to what I know from personal stories and trusted sources like my doctor or community leaders. I believe in balancing facts with empathy, especially since I understand depression and how stressful health talks can be. My approach is to stay calm, ask questions to understand others' views, and gently share why vaccines matter to me, without pushing too hard. It's about building trust and keeping the conversation open, so everyone feels heard.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 18 and occupation as hairdresser suggest frequent social interactions, influencing discussion management. Gender non-binary and Christian beliefs add perspective on inclusivity and care. Widowed status and depression relate to personal health concerns, emphasizing empathy. No social media use implies reliance on direct conversations. High extraversion supports engagement in talks, moderate agreeableness allows for respectful disagreement. Hobbies like running tie to health advocacy. No formal education leads to simple, experience-based communication. Overall, focused on listening, sharing personal stories, and balancing facts with emotional sensitivity.
response_parsed.tone: Conversational, empathetic, and straightforward, reflecting a young adult's perspective with personal anecdotes and a focus on real-world interactions.
